meta.title: Kitsoki dev-story example — PRD to validated result

theme.name: rose-pine-moon

personas.name: Priya
personas.intro: Turns a rough feature idea into a validated PRD and a shareable mockup.

personas.name: Arden
personas.intro: Converts requirements into a design artifact with slices, decisions, and constraints.

personas.name: Andy
personas.intro: Decomposes the design, runs implementation and bugfix loops, and ships with evidence.

scenes.title: Kitsoki dev-story
scenes.subtitle: PRD → design → validated result; one small feature is just the specimen
scenes.narration: This is a Kitsoki dev-story example: one small feature moving from product idea, to design, to work plan, to validated result. The pet is just the specimen. The point is the traceable development loop.

scenes.lede: The feature is not the headline.
scenes.body: The deck uses a deliberately small UI idea so the operating model stays visible: where work enters, what each role produces, which gates run, and how every artifact comes back to the thread that requested it.
scenes.narration: The feature is intentionally small. That lets the deck show the operating model: where work enters, what each role produces, which gates run, and how every artifact returns to the requesting thread.

scenes.title: One story, three roles
scenes.caption: Product, architecture, and engineering share one traceable pipeline instead of handing off disconnected documents.
scenes.narration: The same story passes through product, architecture, and engineering. Kitsoki keeps those hand-offs connected instead of turning them into disconnected documents.

scenes.title: What dev-story proves
scenes.narration: The point of the dev-story is the repeatable contract: intake from the place work already lives, validated artifacts at each phase, gated implementation, and a final result with proof.

cards.label: Work starts where it lives
cards.sub: an issue, mention, or operator action can launch the right story

cards.label: Each phase emits an artifact
cards.sub: PRD, mockup, design, work plan, bug report, fix report, demo

cards.label: Validation is explicit
cards.sub: structural checks, focused tests, replay evidence, and review gates are part of the run

cards.label: The thread stays the source of truth
cards.sub: status, hand-offs, links, and close-out all land back on the same work surface

scenes.title: The SDLC contract
scenes.narration: The phases are simple: product frames the problem, architecture turns requirements into a design, engineering decomposes the work, implementation runs with gates, and the close-out carries proof.

cells: problem framing, requirements, guardrails, and mockup
slices, data boundaries, decisions, and constraints
diff, tests, replay evidence, demo, and PR-ready close-out

scenes.title: 1 · Idea → PRD

cases.action: Starts from a rough, human-sized feature idea
cases.detail: the example happens to be a trace-column pet

cases.action: Lets Kitsoki ask the clarifying questions
cases.detail: scope, constraints, success signal, and non-goals

cases.action: Publishes the PRD plus a mockup artifact
cases.detail: the next role has something concrete to inspect

scenes.caption: The product phase is useful because it leaves a validated artifact, not because the sample feature is important.
scenes.narration: The product phase starts from a rough feature idea, lets Kitsoki ask clarifying questions, and publishes a PRD plus a mockup artifact. The value is the validated output.

scenes.title: From rough idea to PRD
scenes.caption: The sample input is small, but the pattern is the point: intake → clarification → drafted PRD → published artifact.
scenes.narration: The run starts with a small idea. Kitsoki turns that into a structured intake: clarifying questions, answers, a drafted PRD, and a published artifact.

scenes.title: PRD as a replayable deck
scenes.caption: The PRD becomes a self-contained Slidey artifact the team can review without rerunning the conversation.
scenes.narration: The PRD is not trapped in the chat. It becomes a self-contained deck the team can review and replay without rerunning the conversation.

scenes.title: Mockup as inspectable evidence
scenes.caption: The mockup gives the next phase a concrete shape to critique before design work starts.
scenes.narration: The mockup gives the next phase something concrete to inspect. This is what keeps the next hand-off from being just prose.

scenes.title: The artifact returns to GitHub
scenes.caption: @kitsoki claims the issue, runs the PRD story, and replies on the thread with the validated output.
scenes.narration: The work returns to the place it started. Kitsoki claims the issue, runs the PRD story, and reports back on the thread with the validated output attached.

scenes.title: 2 · PRD → design

cases.action: Reads the PRD as source material
cases.detail: notified when product output is ready

cases.action: Turns requirements into slices and decisions

cases.detail: the next phase has explicit constraints

scenes.caption: The design phase changes the question from “what should exist?” to “what should be built, in what shape, with what constraints?”
scenes.narration: Architecture reads the PRD, turns it into slices and decisions, and publishes a design that the developer can decompose. The feature details are secondary to that phase boundary.

scenes.title: Design is a hand-off artifact
scenes.narration: The design artifact is useful because it names the implementation slices, records decisions, and makes constraints explicit before code is written.

cards.label: Slices
cards.sub: right-sized areas of change the developer can reason about

cards.label: Decisions
cards.sub: why the design chose this shape and rejected alternatives

cards.label: Constraints
cards.sub: performance, accessibility, dependencies, and rollout posture

scenes.title: Design, published for engineering
scenes.caption: The design artifact carries slices, boundaries, and decisions forward; the sample UI detail is just the worked example.
scenes.narration: The design artifact carries slices, boundaries, and decisions forward. The sample UI detail is just the worked example.

scenes.title: 3 · Design → work plan

cases.action: Consumes the design as the contract

cases.detail: each task has a gate and a done condition

cases.action: Loads a validated plan for execution

scenes.caption: The decomposition is the bridge from “a design exists” to “agents can safely execute bounded tasks.”
scenes.narration: The developer consumes the design as a contract, decomposes it into dependency-ordered briefs, and validates the plan before background execution starts.

scenes.title: Decomposition returned on the issue
scenes.caption: @kitsoki replies with the validated work-plan deck: tasks, dependencies, and gates are visible before build starts.
scenes.narration: The decomposition returns on the issue as a validated work-plan deck: tasks, dependencies, and gates visible before build starts.

scenes.title: Before code starts
scenes.narration: The plan is not just a list of tasks. It proves that scope, order, and validation are clear enough for execution.

cells: bounded files or components, with explicit non-goals
dependencies are sequenced so each task can stand alone
focused validation is named before implementation starts
close-out says what changed and points to proof

scenes.lede: Bugs use the same story discipline.
scenes.body: A dev-story cannot assume the world stays still. In this run, a UI bug arrives mid-stream. Kitsoki captures evidence, files the issue, triages scope, fixes it, validates the diff, and reports back through the same work surface.
scenes.narration: A dev-story cannot assume the world stays still. Here, a UI bug arrives mid-stream. Kitsoki captures evidence, files the issue, triages scope, fixes it, validates the diff, and reports back through the same work surface.

scenes.title: Bug report with replay evidence
scenes.caption: The operator files a bug from the web UI with the captured session replay attached before it ever becomes a ticket.
scenes.narration: The bug starts in the web UI. The operator reviews the captured replay, describes the problem, and files it with evidence attached.

scenes.title: Bug report and fix report on the thread
scenes.caption: @kitsoki claims the bug issue and replies with both the analysis and the validated fix report.
scenes.narration: On GitHub, Kitsoki claims the bug issue and reports back with the analysis and the validated fix report. The bugfix loop has the same artifact discipline as the feature loop.

scenes.title: Implementation stays gated

cards.label: Reproduce
cards.sub: capture or test the symptom before changing code

cards.label: Patch narrowly
cards.sub: keep the diff tied to the validated scope

cards.label: Run focused gates
cards.sub: typecheck, build, replay, or project-specific checks

cards.label: Self-review and close out
cards.sub: explain the diff, evidence, and remaining risk

scenes.caption: The same pattern handles planned feature work and interrupting bugs.
scenes.narration: Implementation stays gated: reproduce, patch narrowly, run focused checks, self-review, and close out with evidence.

scenes.title: 5 · Build → validated result

cases.action: Runs gates and captures the result
Opens the PR with the story attached

scenes.caption: The final result matters because it is traceable back to the PRD, design, plan, gates, and demo.
scenes.narration: The validated plan turns into implementation, gates run, the result is captured, and the pull request opens with the story context still intact.

scenes.title: The shipped result, captured live
scenes.caption: A live rrweb capture proves the example feature exists; the important part is that the demo is linked to the story that produced it.
scenes.narration: The shipped result is captured live. The important part is not the pet itself. It is that the demo is linked to the story that produced it.

scenes.title: PR opened with context intact

messages.author: github
messages.body: PR opened with the demo deck — trace-column pet 🐾

scenes.caption: The PR is the end of the dev-story trace: artifact links, validation, demo evidence, and the implementation diff meet in one review surface.
scenes.narration: The pull request closes the loop. Artifact links, validation, demo evidence, and the implementation diff meet in one review surface.
That is the Kitsoki dev-story shape: PRD to design to work plan to validated result, with each step replayable and every hand-off traceable.
scenes.url: every artifact is replayable; every hand-off is traceable
